title: Life-first: Kitty serves Jacob's life before its own development
description: Implements ADR 0016 (docs/adr/0016-life-first-ordering.md). Life projects (job search, benefits, education, health, money) outrank code projects everywhere Kitty suggests or ranks; the daily companion path must run fully on free/cheap model routes. Bounded T1 packets suitable for free workers under the standing rules in docs/NORTH_STAR.md section 4.

packets.title: What's Next ranks life projects above code projects
packets.objective: In next-step generation/selection, projects whose kind is not 'code' (job search, benefits, education, and similar life projects) must be preferred over code projects when both have eligible steps. At most one Kitty-self-development suggestion may surface while any life-project step is available. Follow ADR 0016.
acceptance_criteria: Given fixture projects of kind 'code' and a life kind, both with eligible steps, the generated What's Next surfaces the life project's step first
With one life step and multiple Kitty-development steps eligible, at most one self-development suggestion appears and never above the life step
Behavior with only code projects present is unchanged

packets.title: Projects carry an explicit life/code kind the ranking can trust
packets.objective: Ensure every project record exposes a kind that distinguishes life projects from code projects (the UI already renders 'code'/'admin' chips). Add a migration or default so existing projects (Job search, Benefits searching, benefits-admin, Furthering education) are classified as life, and the projects API returns the kind consistently.
acceptance_criteria: GET /projects returns a kind for every project, with the four existing life projects classified as life (not code)
Creating a project without a kind gets an explicit default, not null
A regression test covers classification of the existing fixtures
python3.12 -m pytest tests/ -q passes for the touched test files

packets.title: Daily companion path never hard-requires a premium model
packets.objective: Add a test-level contract that chat, brief, and next-step default to the cheap/free LiteLLM routes (kitty-default, kitty-default-or, kitty-default-gemini) and degrade gracefully (explicit error, not silent failure) if a premium route is unavailable. Extends tests/test_litellm_config.py.
acceptance_criteria: A test asserts the default model for the daily path resolves to a non-premium route from gateway/litellm_config.yaml
A test asserts premium-route unavailability produces an explicit error or fallback, never a silent empty response

packets.title: Morning brief opens with one concrete life action
packets.objective: The morning brief's first content section must be the top life-project next step (one action, with its why), before news/model/system sections. Reuses the LF-01 ranking; if no life step exists, the brief says so honestly rather than substituting a code task.
acceptance_criteria: With a life step available, the rendered brief's first section contains that step and its why
With no life step available, the first section states that explicitly and no code task is promoted into it
python3.12 -m pytest tests/ -q passes for the touched test files
